mission.title: Install and Configure KubeAI
mission.description: KubeAI is an AI Inference Operator for Kubernetes, designed to serve machine learning models efficiently in production environments. It supports various model types including LLMs, embeddings, and speech-to-text, offering intelligent scaling and optimized routing. Key trade-offs include simplicity and zero dependencies, making it easy to deploy without complex configurations.

steps.title: Add the KubeAI Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeAI Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeAI easily.

```bash

helm repo add kubeai https://charts.kubeai.org

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official KubeAI Helm repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeAI CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required by KubeAI to manage its resources effectively.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/main/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_model_crd.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/main/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_inference_crd.yaml

```

> Installs the Model CRD.
> Installs the Inference CRD.

steps.title: Install KubeAI using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the KubeAI operator in the kube-system namespace, enabling it to manage AI inference workloads.

```bash

helm install kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kube-system --create-namespace --version 0.23.1

```

> Installs KubeAI with the specified version.

steps.title: Create an example Inference resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Inference resource to demonstrate how to use KubeAI for serving a model.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: Inference
metadata:
  name: example-inference
spec:
  model:
    name: example-model
    version: latest
EOF

```

> Creates an example Inference resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: Inference
metadata:
  name: example-inference
spec:
  model:
    name: example-model
    version: latest
EOF

steps.title: Verify KubeAI installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the KubeAI pods to ensure that everything is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Lists all pods in the kube-system namespace to verify KubeAI is running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeAI and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all inference resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kubeai --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete crd models.kubeai.substratus.ai

kubectl delete crd inferences.kubeai.substratus.ai

```

> Uninstalls the KubeAI Helm release.
> Deletes the Model CRD.
> Deletes the Inference CRD.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your resources. This process includes draining any existing pods, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system -o name | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kube-system --version 0.23.1

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Drains all KubeAI pods.
> Upgrades KubeAI to the specified version.
> Verifies that the KubeAI pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kube-system -o name | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRD installation URL may be incorrect or the Kubernetes cluster may not have permissions.

**Fix:**
Verify the CRD URL and ensure you have the necessary permissions to create CRDs.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KubeAI pods are in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration in the Inference resource or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system' and adjust the resource requests accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No matching model found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified model may not be available in the KubeAI catalog.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the model name and version specified in the Inference resource are correct.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeAI Helm release may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall KubeAI using the Helm install command provided in the installation steps.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved load balancing algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see KubeAI pods running with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: Inference
metadata:
  name: example-inference
spec:
  model:
    name: example-model
    version: latest
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.28 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
